data.title: Trump unveils 'Great Healthcare Plan' to lower drug prices, premiums
data.description:
President Trump has finally unveiled his long-awaited plan to reform healthcare, dubbed the "Great Healthcare Plan," that focuses on codifying the "most favored nation" deals for cheaper drugs from pharmas as well as funding a cost-sharing reduction program for health insurance plans.

The GHP also calls for ending kickbacks paid by pharmacy benefit managers to brokerage middlemen.

In addition to codifying the MFN program, the plan also calls for having more prescription drugs available for over-the-counter purchase. "This will lower healthcare costs and increase consumer choice by strengthening price transparency, increasing competition, and reducing the need for costly and time-consuming doctor's visits," according to a White House fact sheet.

Regarding insurance premiums, for those who get their coverage through state health exchanges, subsidy payments would be given to individuals directly instead of insurers to buy a plan of their choosing.         

A cost-sharing reduction program would save taxpayers at least $36B—though over what period is unclear—and cut the most common ACA plan premiums by more than 10%, the White House said, citing the Congressional Budget Office. Details were not provided.     

Insurers will also have to provide information in "Plain English" rather than "industry jargon" to make them more accountable. They will also need to prominently publish rate and coverage comparisons, the percentage of their revenues that are paid out to claims compared to overhead costs and profits, and the frequency of claim denials.

data.title: Paramount fails to get its suit against Warner Bros. expedited: report
data.description:
A Delaware judge refused to expedite Paramount Skydance Corp.’s (PSKY) lawsuit that accused directors of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. (WBD) of misleading investors about a more than $82.7B buyout bid from Netflix Inc. (NFLX), Bloomberg reported on Thursday.

According to the report, Judge Morgan Zurn found that Paramount’s demands didn’t meet legal tests for fast-tracking a hearing into accusations that Warner Bros.' board made erroneous disclosures about the deal.

Paramount failed to show it would "suffer irreparable harm" by Warner Bros.' allegedly deficient disclosures, Zurn said. The judge explained that as a stockholder, Paramount can’t be harmed directly by a lack of disclosures since it won’t be making any decision on its own tender offer.

Paramount filed the suit earlier this week to obtain more details about Warner Bros.' deal with Netflix. The David Ellison-led company wants Warner Bros. to correct misleading information about the deal before investors vote on it.The case is Paramount v. Zaslav, 2026-0044, Delaware Chancery Court (Wilmington).

data.title: Alamos slides as 2025 revenue hit record but gold production missed guidance
data.description:
Alamos Gold (AGI) -6.7% in Thursday's trading after reporting FY 2025 gold production fell 3.8% Y/Y to 545,400 oz, missing revised guidance of 560K-580K oz, and Q4 output of 141,500 oz, roughly flat from 141,700 oz in Q3 and slightly above 140,200 oz in the year-ago quarter.

Alamos (AGI) said lower than expected production from the Island Gold district and Young-Davidson mine weighed on full-year results, and severe weather in December disrupted access to sites and affected mining and processing rates.

Island Gold produced 250,400 oz for the year and 60K oz in Q4, Young-Davidson produced 153,400 oz for the year and 41,500 oz in the quarter, and the Mulatos district contributed 141,600 oz for the year and 40K oz in Q4.

At the same time, Alamos (AGI) set revenue records for FY 2025 and Q4, with full-year sales totaling 531,230 oz of gold at an average realized price of $3,372/oz to generate revenues of $1.8B, and Q4 sales of 142,149 oz of gold at an average realized price of $3,997/oz for revenues of $568M.

"From an operational perspective, this past year was not reflective of our long-term track record, nor our strong outlook," President and CEO John McCluskey said. "We expect a substantial improvement from our operations in 2026 and low-cost growth from the Island Gold district to drive production significantly higher over the next several years."

data.title: Diageo is viewed positively by RBC Capital as the focus shifts to the mainstream part of the business
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets sees upside for Diageo plc (DEO) with the new CEO expected to revitalize the mainstream business. 

Analyst James Edwardes Jones highlighted that Diageo (DEO) enjoys an enviable stable of brands across multiple price points, although it was noted the company has overemphasized its luxury segment, with the brands now becoming increasingly unaffordable. "We think that reviving the neglected mainstream will be the key to Diageo rediscovering sustainable volume growth," updated Edwardes Jones.

Notably, the mainstream part of the alcohol business is where incoming CEO Sir Dave Lewis has spent his career. His lack of experience in the spirits sector is not expected by Edwardes Jones to be an issue as he implements his strategy changes.

Still, Diageo (DEO) is anticipated to see some pressure on price mix and margins, even as plentiful cost-saving opportunities arise. Positive catalysts seen for 2026 include the first-half fiscal year results to be reported in February and a potential Capital Markets Day.

RBC Capital assigned a price target of £20 to DEO to represent more than 16% upside potential.

Shares of Diageo (DEO) turned 1.4% lower in late morning trading.

data.title: State Street said to launch tokenized products in crypto push
data.description:
State Street (STT) is moving deeper into digital assets as the custody bank is developing a suite of tokenized products, according to a Thursday media report.

The company is specifically rolling out money-market and exchange-traded funds and cash products like tokenized deposits and stablecoins, Bloomberg reported, citing an emailed statement.

State Street (STT) will work with its money managers and institutional clients, in addition to its asset management division, Bloomberg reported. That unit last month teamed up with Mike Novogratz's Galaxy Digital (GLXY) to launch a tokenized private liquidity fund. State Street already takes care of administration and accounting for clients' digital-asset holdings and also crypto ETFs.

The move comes as big financial firms are expanding in crypto as the Trump administration take a more supportive stance. BNY (BK), another custody lender, rolled out a money market fund tailored to stablecoin issuers.

State Street (STT) did not immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Bank7 outlines disciplined capital strategy while maintaining strong loan growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Bank7 Corp. (BSVN) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Thomas Travis expressed satisfaction with 2025 results, highlighting "the outstanding loan growth, the strong loan fee income and very solid organic deposit growth," attributing these achievements to the bank's experienced team. Travis emphasized that "they did it without sacrificing underwriting, and that enables us to really enjoy asset quality that is probably better than it's ever been."
  • Travis reinforced operational strength, stating, "our operations, IT, finance functions continue to evolve and they make our lives easy and something we don't take for granted."
  • CFO Kelly Harris remarked, "we had a couple of rate cuts during the quarter. And you could tell in the slides in the deck that we've kind of reached an inflection point where we had a number of loans that reached their floors."

Outlook

  • Jason Estes, Chief Credit Officer, noted that while payoffs were lighter in the fourth quarter, forward growth at the same pace as 2025 "sounds like a stretch to me." He added, "we do think that we could grow loans at a similar pace, but you have to fund that, and you have to maintain those margins."
  • Harris provided guidance on net interest margin (NIM), saying, "I think going within that high band, 4.5 is a great starting point for us." Travis cautioned that further rate cuts could push NIM down to historical lows, stating, "it wouldn't surprise us to dip down and touch our historical lows."
  • Management reiterated a disciplined approach to share buybacks, with Travis stating, "we're just focused on producing top-tier results. And over time, the market will understand that and the stock price will respond."

Financial Results

  • Harris reported, "using Q4 as a really solid guide, I think it was $9.1 million in core expense, $1 million in oil and gas. And then similar on the fee income side, $1 million split, $1 million on the oil and gas and $1 million in core fee income, $2 million in total."
  • Estes discussed the cost of funds, stating, "Our current cost of funds dipped from Q4, I think the current run rate is 2.40."

Q&A

  • Wood Lay, KBW: Questioned if strong loan growth could continue and the outlook for payoffs. Estes replied that "the fourth quarter payoffs were lighter than they'd been in the first, second and third," but growth at 2025's pace "sounds like a stretch."
  • Lay, KBW: Asked about NIM trends with additional rate cuts. Harris answered, "we feel really good about our current NIM. Could it go down slightly? Potentially."
  • Nathan Race, Piper Sandler: Asked about deposit costs and leverage to reduce them. Harris responded, "Our current cost of funds dipped from Q4...helped reduce that cost to fund."
  • Race, Piper Sandler: Asked about deposit pricing competition. Estes noted, "the last couple of cuts didn't really flow into deposit betas as strongly as maybe the first couple."
  • Race, Piper Sandler: Raised the topic of buybacks and capital deployment. Travis stated, "resist the urge to do any meaningful share buyback so that we can pile up capital and just be prepared for a nice opportunity."
  • Jordan Ghent, Stephens: Queried about M&A headwinds. Travis explained, "as it relates to price, the higher quality as the deposit franchise, the long and the two deposit relationships that some banks have, that's going to force you into a higher multiple and there's just nothing you can do about it."
  • Ghent, Stephens: Asked about noninterest-bearing deposits and seasonality. Estes replied, "what you're seeing as those noninterest-bearing accounts, that percentage bleeds down...that's changed with the last rate cycle."
  • Ghent, Stephens: Requested expense and fee guidance. Harris said, "using Q4 as a really solid guide, I think it was $9.1 million in core expense, $1 million in oil and gas...very, very similar to Q4."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst questions frequently focused on sustainability of growth, NIM pressures, and capital deployment, with a neutral to slightly skeptical tone, especially regarding the likelihood of repeating 2025's growth rate and the effectiveness of deposit cost management.
  • Management remained confident in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing discipline and operational strength. Travis and Harris acknowledged margin challenges but maintained a constructive outlook, often expressing confidence in the team's ability and strategy. Key phrases included, "we feel really good about our current NIM" and "we're just focused on producing top-tier results."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, analysts' tone was more focused on the sustainability of strong results and the potential impact of rate cuts, while management was consistent in expressing confidence but became more explicit about margin compression and capital discipline.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted to acknowledge that matching the prior year's loan growth would be "a stretch," whereas the previous quarter targeted "high single-digit year-over-year growth."
  • Strategic focus remains on organic growth and capital accumulation, but there is increased emphasis on maintaining discipline amid heightened deposit competition and margin pressure.
  • Analysts' questions this quarter concentrated more on margin sustainability, capital deployment, and M&A headwinds, compared to broader growth and credit topics last quarter.
  • Key metrics such as NIM are now discussed with more caution, recognizing likely downward trends due to rate cuts.
  • Management's confidence in their strategy and operational execution persisted, but language reflected a more measured outlook regarding growth and profitability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited pricing pressure on loans and deposits, with Estes noting, "where we're seeing the most pressure is pricing wise, and we are not going to lose our discipline."
  • Interest rate cuts and their impact on NIM and deposit costs were highlighted as ongoing challenges, with Travis stating the possibility of margins dipping to historical lows.
  • The competitive deposit environment and customer rate sensitivity were flagged by Estes as "top of mind awareness" for depositors, increasing competition for funding.
  • M&A remains difficult due to high pricing expectations and quality deposit franchise scarcity, with Travis explaining, "it's just increasingly difficult, and the market is a mature market."

Final Takeaway

Bank7 Corp. closed 2025 with robust loan and deposit growth, supported by strong asset quality and disciplined underwriting. Management signaled that while similar levels of growth may be difficult to replicate in 2026, the company is focused on maintaining margins, controlling expenses, and accumulating capital for strategic opportunities. Despite headwinds from rate cuts and heightened deposit competition, the bank remains committed to operational discipline and long-term shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: India warns Apple it will proceed in antitrust case: report
data.description:
The Indian antitrust regulator has issued a final warning to Apple (AAPL) that it will proceed in a case as the company has delayed responses to officials for over a year and undermined the probe, Reuters reported, citing a confidential order.

Apple has said it is concerned that it could be fined up to $38B if India's competition watchdog uses its global turnover calculation for fines, after an investigation found it had abused its position on its app store, the report added.

Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Apple, which denies the allegations, has challenged the penalty rules in an Indian court, and the matter is pending, according to a report from November 2025. The challenge is the first against India's antitrust penalty law that since 2024, enables the Competition Commission of India, or CCI, to use global turnover when calculating fines it imposes on companies for abusing their market dominance.

While the Delhi High Court is still hearing that challenge, a confidential Dec. 31, 2025, order from the CCI shows Apple privately sought to halt the entire case while the penalty‑rule dispute is before the court. The CCI rejected the request, the report added.

The antitrust agency said it asked Apple in October 2024 to submit objections to the probe's findings and provide financial details used to evaluate fines, but the company has received "repeated extensions" since then, according to the report.

"The Commission is of the considered view that repeated extensions, despite unambiguous directions, undermine procedural discipline and impede the timely conclusion of proceedings," CCI noted in its order, the report added.

"Such indulgence cannot be continued indefinitely," said the order, giving Apple a final warning that it will proceed in the case unilaterally if no response is received by next week, the report noted.

A source familiar with the matter told the news agency that Apple views CCI's December 2025 order as a move to preempt the ongoing court proceedings, and the company was not likely to respond to it before the judges hear it on Jan. 27, the report added.

Since 2022, Tinder-owner Match (MTCH) and Indian startups have been in an antitrust tussle with Apple at the CCI. In July 2024 it was reported that a probe by the CCI allegedly found that Apple exploited its dominant position in the app store market on iOS, engaging "in abusive conduct and practices."

data.title: AI semiconductor market forecasts to reach over $550B by 2028: RBC
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets expects semiconductor revenue stemming from artificial intelligence applications to grow from $220B in 2025 to more than $550B by 2028.

"Tight supply is leading to extended visibility with managements highlighting 18-month backlogs," said RBC analysts Srini Pajjuri and Grant Li in an investor report. "Infrastructure constraints could delay some projects but aren't necessarily a negative in our view, as they may ultimately extend and smooth the AI spending cycle. We see limited threat to GPU dominance despite the recent custom ASIC progress given the rapid pace of AI technology evolution and long ASIC design cycles."

This backdrop prompted the financial firm to initiate coverage on an array of semiconductor companies with an Outperform rating. This included Nvidia (NVDA), Micron (MU), Marvell (MRVL), Arm (ARM), Astera Labs (ALAB), ASML (ASML), Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX) and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC).

RBC applied Sector Perform ratings to Broadcom (AVGO), AMD (AMD), Intel (INTC), KLA (KLAC), Sandisk (SNDK), Qualcomm (QCOM), Skyworks Solutions (SWKS) and Silicon Laboratories (SLAB).

High-bandwidth memory demand is expected to be a key driver and should reduce the cyclical nature of the memory market.

"AI workloads are shifting to reinforced learning and distributed inferencing, which are Memory intensive," Pajjuri noted. "Upcoming HBM4 transition is another material tailwind given 30-50% ASP premium. GenAI is also driving strong demand for high-capacity server DIMMs and NAND eSSDs. While high Memory prices could have an impact on PC/Smartphone demand, we expect Memory demand to outpace supply into 2027."

Like several other financial firms have indicated, wafer fabrication equipment spending is also expected to be strong over the next two years.

"In addition, technology trends such as back-side power, advanced packaging, and 3D structures give us conviction that WFE can grow at least at a high single-digit rate for the next 2 years," Pajjuri added.

data.title: Gap broadens "fashiontainment" strategy with newly created Chief Entertainment Officer role
data.description:
Gap (GAP) is entering the “Fashiontainment” arena and broadening its engagement with customers through a newly-created position of Chief Entertainment Officer, tapping Paramount’s (PSKY) CEO of International Markets, Global Consumer Products and Experiences Pam Kaufman to spearhead the new initiative.

Kaufman will report directly to Gap (GAP) CEO Richard Dickson.

This new role is designed to build and scale Gap’s (GAP) entertainment content, and licensing platform across a broad range of media, including music, TV, gaming, and sports, “championing innovative storytelling to unlock value at the intersection of fashion and entertainment,” the company said.

To embed the new division to the entertainment industry, Gap (GAP) will establish an office on Los Angeles’ Sunset Strip.  

"As we reinvigorate Gap Inc.'s house of iconic American brands to drive relevance and revenue, we recognize entertainment is a critical link to the consumer. Through Fashiontainment, we are unlocking that potential to take our brands to the next level,” Dickson said in a statement.

Gap (GAP) hopes to repeat the success of its “Better in Denim” campaign with Katseye. The campaign, “is striking a range between being one of the most iconic brand campaigns. People aren’t just watching, they’re actively joining,” Dickson said recently in an interview.

By expanding its reach into the entertainment forum, fashion retailers can bypass traditional advertising and connect with an audience that is not exposed – mostly by choice – to TV and print ads. The strategy also establishes a brand as more of a “lifestyle” than just a product by incorporating storytelling and branded entertainment.

More crucially, by strengthening a brand’s cachet retailers are able to command premium pricing and limit the need for frequent promotional pricing.

The news has stalled Gap’s (GAP) recent downtrend of four straight losses with a gain of more than 1% on Thursday.

data.title: Senator wants answers from BNY on $378M of Epstein transfers
data.description:
Senator Ron Wyden (D-OR), the top Democrat on the Senate Finance Committee, is seeking information from BNY (BK) about $378M in transfers for Jeffrey Epstein's BNY accounts as the committee investigates the financing of Epstein's sex-trafficking activities, according to a letter the senator sent to the bank on Thursday.

He expressed concern that the bank wasn't sufficiently vigilant in flagging suspicious activity related to the accounts. Treasury Department files documenting the flow of money through Epstein's accounts included a 2019 filing by BNY that flagged 280 suspicious wire transfers in and out of Epstein's accounts at BNY, the senator said.

Wyden noted that documents reviewed by the Finance Committee found that BNY (BK) reported the suspicious transactions to the U.S. government only after Epstein was arrested on federal sex-trafficking charges.

"This raises serious concerns that BNY may have violated federal anti-money laundering laws that require financial institutions to conduct due diligence on customer relationships and report suspicious activity to the U.S. government in a timely fashion," Wyden wrote in the letter.

Specifically, he said BNY (BK) processed and assisted in the transfer of at least $20M in 2007 (all ~$1M amounts multiple times a month) to Epstein's accounts at JPMorgan Chase (JPM), a pattern "indicative of potential money laundering."

Wyden's letter demanded copies of all suspicious activity reports filed by BNY from Epstein and his associates, copies of all know-your-customer and customer due diligence profiles prepared by BNY Mellon for Epstein between 2003 and 2019, and a list of all cash withdrawals exceeding $10K made from Epstein's accounts at BNY between 2001 and 2019, with the stated purpose of each of those transactions. 

He also asked whether BNY Mellon filed any SARs with the Treasury Department before Epstein's arrest in July 2019 and whether the bank ever conducted an internal investigation into its management of Epstein's accounts.

A BNY spokesperson pointed to comments the company made related to litigation against the bank for its alleged relationship with Epstein. "As we have made clear, Jeffrey Epstein was never a direct client of BNY, and we will continue to defend ourselves against these meritless allegations," the company said.

Furthermore, the company stated, "Jeffrey Epstein committed crimes that deserve to be fully investigated and prosecuted. We recognize and appreciate the focus on accountability for those acts."

In its motion to dismiss the complaint, BNY said, "Across 216 paragraphs, the complaint never identifies a single BNY employee, or even a division of BNY, with whom Epstein supposedly dealt."

BNY (BK) rose 0.3% in Thursday morning trading.
